arts.ttl: Unai Emery says PSG’s third goal does not change Villa’s task in second leg
arts.sum: Nuno Mendes’ added-time goal saw Villa lose 3-1 in Paris.

arts.ttl: William takes eldest son George to watch Villa in Paris
arts.sum: The Prince of Wales says it was a "big deal" for him to take his son Prince George to Paris to watch Aston Villa face Paris St-Germain in the Champions League quarter-finals.

arts.ttl: ‘Two goals is nothing’ – PSG’s Nuno Mendes wary of Aston Villa threat
arts.sum: Paris Saint-Germain are in the driving seat in their UEFA Champions League quarter-final against Aston Villa thanks to a 3-1 win at the Parc des Princes on Wednesday night. However,…

arts.ttl: Villa's formation gamble didn't pay off - analysis
arts.sum: Match of the Day pundit Stephen Warnock explains how Aston Villa's defensive change from a back five in the first half to a back three in the second half proved to be costly for the Premier League side in their 3-1 loss to PSG in their Champions League quarter-final first leg at the Parc Des Princes.

arts.ttl: Why PSG can go all the way to Champions League final
arts.sum: Thomas Hitzlsperger believes PSG will get to the Champions League final, saying that the French Champions now look like a team rather than a group of individuals as they have done in the past.

arts.ttl: Paul Merson shares whether he thinks Aston Villa can now overturn 3-1 deficit against PSG in next week's clash
arts.sum: Aston Villa were beaten 3-1 by a clinical Paris Saint-Germain side in Wednesday’s Champions League quarter-final first-leg at the Parc des Princes. The Villans took...

arts.ttl: Kylian Mbappe has made Real Madrid the new PSG
arts.sum: Definitive proof of this fact arrived on Tuesday evening at the Emirates Stadium as Carlo Ancelotti’s side submitted meekly to a 3-0 loss against Arsenal . Arsenal outworked their guests by an enormous margin of 12km in total, while not a single Real Madrid player ran more than 10km, according to Relevo .

arts.ttl: ‘My first thought was to cross’ – PSG’s Désiré Doué on his wondergoal against Aston Villa
arts.sum: Désiré Doué (19) was the man who made the difference for Paris Saint-Germain as they beat Aston Villa at the Parc des Princes on Wednesday, putting them in the driving…

arts.ttl: Rio Ferdinand names new Champions League favourite after Arsenal and PSG wins
arts.sum: PSG saw off Aston Villa in the first-leg of their Champions League quarter-final, having also dispatched Liverpool, with Rio Ferdinand declaring them his favourites to win the tournament

arts.ttl: Morgan Rogers insists Aston Villa ‘not done yet’ despite defeat to ‘deadly’ PSG
arts.sum: Rogers opened the scoring before the hosts flexed their attacking muscles to seize a first-leg advantage
